Memory layout + chroma schema.question: .voss/memory/ on-disk shape
Memory layout + chroma schema.answer: Per-source dirs of files (turns/<session>.jsonl, decisions/, conventions/*.md, ledgers/*.jsonl); chroma persists alongside as index, not source of truth

Memory layout + chroma schema.question: Chroma collection topology
Memory layout + chroma schema.answer: Claude discretion — default single collection voss_memory with source_type metadata; reconsider per-source only if Req 3 top-3 hit rate forces it

Memory layout + chroma schema.question: Turn-content indexing granularity
Memory layout + chroma schema.answer: Per-turn (one chroma entry per user/assistant turn)

VOSS.md sections + COG-02 rewrite.question: Machine-vs-human section markers in VOSS.md
VOSS.md sections + COG-02 rewrite.answer: HTML-comment fences: <!-- voss:begin id=<slug> --> ... <!-- voss:end id=<slug> -->. Multiple machine blocks supported by distinct ids.

VOSS.md sections + COG-02 rewrite.question: Where pre-M8 architecture.md content lands in new VOSS.md
VOSS.md sections + COG-02 rewrite.answer: Inside the `voss:begin id=architecture` machine fence so future COG-02 runs rewrite the same block. Archive original to .voss/archive/architecture-YYYY-MM-DD.md byte-identical (sha256 verified).

VOSS.md sections + COG-02 rewrite.question: Conflict handling when machine fence has been touched
VOSS.md sections + COG-02 rewrite.answer: Refuse + surface diff. Last-written hash stored in `<!-- voss:hash <sha> -->` line inside the fence; on rewrite, if on-disk hash differs, abort the write and tell user to run `voss memory adopt` (or equivalent) to accept the human edits as the new baseline.

VOSS.md sections + COG-02 rewrite.question: VOSS.md injection shape into system context
VOSS.md sections + COG-02 rewrite.answer: Single labeled block injecting full file bytes verbatim (matches Req 1 acceptance). One block, no machine/human split at injection time.
